title: An Initial Design for Generative Game Mechanics via LLM Function Emulation
venue: Proceedings of the 17th International Conference on Computational Creativity (Forthcoming).
blurb: Using a large language model to compute game state updates instead of hardcoded mechanics.

title: A Development and Teaching Framework for Codenames
venue: Proceedings of the 16th International Conference on Computational Creativity, 239-247.
blurb: A multiplayer Codenames engine supporting human and AI palyers with HTML and REST clients.

title: Constraints as Catalysts: A (De) Construction of Codenames as a Creative Task
venue: Proceedings of the 14th International Conference on Computational Creativity, 337-341.
blurb: Calculating the number of possible Codenames clues to analyze the creativity of the spymaster task.

title: What Makes Gameplay Creative?
venue: Proceedings of the 14th International Conference on Computational Creativity, 98-101.
blurb: A game admits creative play if players choose from a large set of meaningful moves in a deterministic game.

title: Competitive Language Games as Creative Tasks with Well-Defined Goals
venue: Proceedings of the 13th International Conference on Computational Creativity, 291–299.
blurb: A creative system for playing Codenames and analysis of computational creative gameplay.

title: Humans in the Black Box: A New Paradigm for Evaluating the Design of Creative Systems
venue: Proceedings of the 11th International Conference on Computational Creativity, 311–318.
blurb: Proposing using human participants to compute creative module output to validate creative system design.

title: Creating Six-word Stories via Inferred Linguistic and Semantic Formats
venue: Proceedings of the 11th International Conference on Computational Creativity, 123–130.
blurb: Expanded creative system for writing six-word stories using word embeddings to mimic human stories.

title: Modeling Knowledge, Expression, and Aesthetics via Sensory Grounding
venue: Proceedings of the 10th International Conference on Computational Creativity, 326–330.
blurb: A theoretical analysis of evaluating and communicating concepts built upon sensory input.

title: An HBPL-based Approach to the Creation of Six-word Stories
venue: Proceedings of the 9th International Conference on Computational Creativity, 161–168.
blurb: Initial design for a creative system that writes six-word stories in a fixed format.

authors: Ruoti, S., Andersen, J., Dickinson, L., Heidbrink, S., Monson, T., O'neill, M., Reese, K. Vaziripour, E., Wu, J., Zappala, D. and Seamons, K.
title: A Usability Study of Four Secure Email Tools Using Paired Participants
venue: ACM Transactions on Privacy and Security (TOPS), 22(2), 13.
